zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Leighton於1350年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾麗頓時，他的父親John de Leighton30歲，母親Matilda Cambray20歲。他和Elizabeth Stapleton至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1454年3月4日享年104歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Leighton于1350年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾麗頓时，他的父亲John de Leighton30岁，母亲Matilda Cambray20岁。他和Elizabeth Stapleton至少有4个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1454年3月4日享年104岁。

nameMeanings.name: Leighton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Leighton or Layton. Most, as for example those in Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire, Cheshire, Lancashire, and Shropshire, are named with Old English lēac ‘leek’ + tūn ‘settlement’, but Layton in Durham derives from Old English ge-lād ‘road’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The English pronunciation of Leighton is usually /lɛ:tən/ or /leitən/ (also spelled Layton and Leaton ), but it can also be heard as /laitən/ or as /li:tən/ (also spelled Leaton ), and occasionally as /lɛtən/ (spelled Letton ).

variants.name: Slayton
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Leighton (compare Layton ) with the addition of initial S-. Alternatively, perhaps a variant of Sladen or of Sleightholme, principally a habitational name from Sleightholme Dale in Kirkbymoorside, but perhaps occasionally from Sleightholme in Bowes or the lost Sleightholme in Lythe. The placenames derive from Old Norse sléttr ‘smooth, level’ + holmr ‘small island, water meadow’. This surname is now very rare in Britain. Compare Slaydon .

variants.name: Laughton
meanings: habitational name from Laughton en le Morthen in Yorkshire, Laughton in Leicestershire or any of the two places called Laughton in Lincolnshire (one near Gainsborough and another near Folkingham). The places are named with Old English lēac ‘leek’ + tūn ‘enclosure’, meaning ‘village or farm where leeks are grown’. It may sometimes have alternated with Leighton , which is mostly from placenames with the same etymology.

variants.name: Letton
meanings: English: habitational name, most probably from Letton in Norfolk or Latton in Essex. It could also be a variant of Leighton or Laughton . Most of the places so named have the same etymology as Letton and Latton, i.e. ‘farm where leeks are grown’ (Old English lēac-tūn). Another possibility is that it is a variant of Latten .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Leighton was born in 1350, in Leighton, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John de Leighton, was 30 and his mother, Matilda Cambray, was 20. He had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Elizabeth Stapleton. He died on 4 March 1454, at the age of 104.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Leighton est né en 1350, à Leighton, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Leighton, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Matilda Cambray, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Elizabeth Stapleton. Il est décédé le 4 mars 1454, à l’âge de 104 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Leighton 1350 in Leighton, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Leighton 30 und seine Mutter Matilda Cambray 20. Er hatte mit Elizabeth Stapleton mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 4. März 1454 im Alter von 104 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Leighton è nato il nel 1350 nel Leighton, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John de Leighton, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Matilda Cambray, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie con Elizabeth Stapleton. Morì il 4 marzo 1454, all'età di 104 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Leighton(이)가 1350년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 레이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Leighton(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Cambray(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Elizabeth Stapleton(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1454년 3월 4일 104세의 나이로 사망했다.
